questions.q: Under standard conditions, the density of a gas is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1.3</mn><mtext> </mtext><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">mg cm</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math> and the velocity of propagation of sound, in it, is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>330</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The number of degrees of freedom of gas is
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A system consists of two types of gas molecules <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> having the same number density <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>25</mn></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The diameter of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mi>A</mi></math> respectively. They suffer collisions at room temperature. The ratio of average distance covered by the molecule <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> to that of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> between two successive collisions is ___________<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In an ideal gas at temperature <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi></math>, the average force that a molecule applies on the walls of a closed container depends on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi></math> as <math><msup><mrow><mi>T</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>q</mi></mrow></msup></math>. A good estimate for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>q</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
The pressure exerted by molecules of a gas on the walls p=13nmc2

∴ p∝c2

But C∝T and C2∝T

∴    P∝T

∵    Force =P×A

∴    F∝T1

q=1
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
